metadata.title: Guideline on adjustment for baseline covariates in clinical trials

rules.rule_text: All covariates to be included in the primary analysis model must be pre-specified in the study protocol before unblinding or database lock.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any covariates to be included in the primary analysis should be pre-specified in the study protocol.

rules.rule_text: Stratification variables used in randomisation must be included as covariates in the primary analysis model; failure to do so can result in an inflated Type I error.
rules.verbatim_quote: If stratification is used in the randomisation, the stratification variables should be included as covariates in the primary analysis. Omitting stratification variables from the analysis can result in an inflated type I error.

rules.rule_text: Observed baseline imbalance between treatment groups is NOT an appropriate reason to add covariates to the primary analysis model post hoc; doing so is not acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: Observed baseline imbalance between treatment groups is not an appropriate reason to include a covariate in the primary analysis if it was not pre-specified.

rules.rule_text: Post-randomisation measurements must not be included as covariates in the primary analysis because they may be influenced by treatment, introducing bias.
rules.verbatim_quote: Post-randomisation measurements should not be used as covariates in the primary analysis as they could be influenced by the treatment, which would introduce bias.

rules.rule_text: When the primary outcome is a continuous variable, the baseline value of that outcome variable should be included as a covariate in the primary analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the primary outcome is a continuous variable, the baseline value of this variable should be included as a covariate in the primary analysis.
rules.exceptions: Not applicable when the baseline value is not measured or when the study design precludes its use.

rules.rule_text: The number of covariates included in the primary analysis should be small (no more than a few); overfitting the model with many covariates is not acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: The number of covariates included in the primary analysis should be small. Including too many covariates may lead to model overfitting.

rules.rule_text: Data-driven variable selection methods (e.g., stepwise regression) must not be used to select covariates in confirmatory clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Data-driven methods for selecting covariates, such as stepwise regression, should not be used in confirmatory clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: The validity of assumptions of the chosen covariate adjustment model must be checked and the findings documented in the clinical study report.
rules.verbatim_quote: The validity of the assumptions of the model should be checked and the findings documented in the clinical study report.

rules.rule_text: Pre-planned sensitivity analyses exploring the impact of covariate adjustment choices must be presented; unadjusted analyses should be included as sensitivity analyses when adjusted analyses are primary.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sensitivity analyses that examine the impact of different covariate adjustment choices should be pre-planned and presented. When the primary analysis is adjusted, an unadjusted analysis should be presented as a sensitivity analysis.

rules.rule_text: Statistical testing for baseline imbalance (balance tests) has no role in assessing the success of randomisation in a properly randomised clinical trial and should not be used to decide whether covariates need to be included.
rules.verbatim_quote: Statistical testing for baseline imbalance has no role to play in assessing the success of randomisation in a properly randomised clinical trial and should not be used to select covariates for inclusion in the primary analysis.

rules.rule_text: For dynamic allocation (minimisation) methods, the implications for Type I error control must be carefully considered; permutation-based re-randomisation test methods should be considered as a valid approach.
rules.verbatim_quote: When dynamic allocation (minimisation) is used, the implications for type I error control must be carefully considered. Permutation-based re-randomisation test methods may be considered.

rules.rule_text: In multicentre trials, stratification variables used for randomisation (including centre or pooled centre groupings) must be adjusted for in the primary analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: In multicentre trials, the stratification variables used for randomisation, including centre or groups of centres, should be adjusted for in the primary analysis.
rules.exceptions: Centres may be pooled when individual centre numbers are too small.

rules.rule_text: Missing covariate data must not be handled by excluding patients from the primary analysis, as this violates the intention-to-treat (ITT) principle; the approach to missing covariate values must be addressed at the planning stage in accordance with the missing data guideline.
rules.verbatim_quote: Ignoring patients with missing covariate information violates the intention-to-treat principle and is therefore not acceptable. The approach to missing covariate information should be addressed at the planning stage.
